get.description: Returns a paginated list of all admin accounts. Super admin only.

post.description: Creates a new admin account. Only super_admin can perform this action. Role defaults to support if not provided.

patch.description: Updates an admin's full_name, role, or is_active status. Super admin only.

get.description: Returns all ad campaigns across all users with pagination and optional filters.

patch.description: Allows admin to force-activate, suspend, or cancel any campaign.
Sets the daily ad price for artisans and/or property owners. Requires super_admin or admin role.

get.description: Returns platform-wide ad revenue, campaign counts, and analytics.
Returns a paginated list of admin audit logs. Filterable by admin_id, action keyword, and entity_type.

parameters.description: Filter by entity type e.g. user, booking, job
parameters.name: entity_type

post.description: Authenticates an admin with email and password. Returns a JWT token valid for 24 hours.
Clears the admin Bearer cookie.

get.description: Returns the profile of the currently authenticated admin.

patch.description: Updates the authenticated admin's password. Requires the current password.

post.description: Issues a new JWT for an admin using a valid refresh token cookie. Rotates the refresh token.

get.description: Returns aggregate platform statistics: user counts by role, booking counts, job counts, wallet volume, and new user trends.
Returns live card metrics: total users, active artisans online, active bookings, total escrow funds held, and open disputes.
Returns job stats (active, completed, cancelled, disputed) and a paginated job list. Optionally filter by status.
Returns full detail for a single artisan job including the timeline of all events (requests, quotes, disputes).

post.description: Registers a device FCM token for an admin to receive push notifications. device_type must be android or ios.

get.description: Returns a paginated list of disputes across all types (job, booking, order) with aggregate stats. Filter by type and/or status.

parameters.description: Dispute type: job, booking, order (omit for all)
parameters.name: type

parameters.description: Filter by status: open, investigating, resolved_refund, resolved_release, dismissed
parameters.name: status

get.description: Returns full detail for a single dispute including parties, reason, evidence, escrow snapshot, admin notes, and resolution. Supply the dispute type via the `type` query param.

parameters.description: Dispute type: job, booking, or order (default: job)
parameters.name: type

post.description: Issues a final decision on a dispute. Depending on the chosen action, funds held in escrow are refunded to the payer, released to the payee, or split. The dispute and parent record (job / booking / order) are updated accordingly and both parties are notified.

parameters.description: Dispute type: job, booking, or order (default: job)
parameters.name: type

400.description: Invalid action, amount, or dispute already resolved

patch.description: Sets the status of a dispute to `open` or `investigating` and optionally attaches admin notes. Use this to flag that a dispute is being looked into before issuing a final decision.

parameters.description: Dispute type: job, booking, or order (default: job)
parameters.name: type

get.description: Returns the support ticket that was created for the given dispute together with the full chronological message history. Returns 404 if no ticket has been created yet.
get.summary: Get the support ticket linked to a dispute (admin)

parameters.description: Dispute type: job, booking, or order (default: job)
parameters.name: type

404.description: No ticket found for this dispute

post.description: Creates a support ticket linked to the specified dispute and assigns it to the calling admin, changing the dispute status to `investigating`. If a ticket already exists for the dispute, the existing ticket ID is returned without creating a duplicate. The user who filed the dispute receives a push notification and an in-app notification inviting them to the support chat.
post.summary: Escalate a dispute to a live-chat support ticket (admin)

parameters.description: Dispute type: job, booking, or order (default: job)
parameters.name: type

parameters.description: Ticket options (subject and priority are optional)
parameters.name: body

get.description: Returns a paginated list of artisan jobs. Filterable by status, artisan_id, and client_id.
Returns all platform configuration settings.

patch.description: Updates the value of a platform configuration key. Super admin only.

get.description: Returns a paginated list of all support tickets across all users, sorted by priority (urgent → high → medium → low) then by most recently updated. Includes per-ticket unread and message counts, plus global open/in-progress/resolved stats. Accessible by super_admin, admin, and support roles.

parameters.description: Filter by status: open | assigned | in_progress | waiting_user | resolved | closed
parameters.name: status

parameters.description: Filter by category: payment_and_refund | booking_and_reservation | service_issues | disputes | account_and_verification | technical_issue | report_user_or_property | general_inquiry | other
parameters.name: category

parameters.description: Filter by priority: low | medium | high | urgent
parameters.name: priority

get.description: Returns full ticket detail including user name, email, assigned admin name, message count, and a paginated message history (oldest-first). Also marks all unread user messages as read. Accessible by super_admin, admin, and support roles.
get.summary: Get a single support ticket with messages (admin)

patch.description: Sets assigned_admin_id on the ticket and moves status to 'assigned'. Restricted to super_admin and admin roles only (support agents cannot reassign tickets).
patch.summary: Assign a support ticket to an admin (admin)

parameters.description: UUID of the admin to assign
parameters.name: body

get.description: Returns paginated messages for a ticket oldest-first (up to 200 per page). Also marks all unread user messages in the ticket as read. Accessible by super_admin, admin, and support roles.
get.summary: Get message history for a support ticket (admin)

post.description: Saves a text message from an admin into the ticket, auto-assigns the ticket to the replying admin if not yet assigned, advances status from open/assigned → in_progress, delivers the message over WebSocket to both the user and the admin, and sends a push notification + in-app notification to the user. Accessible by super_admin, admin, and support roles.
post.summary: Send a text reply to a support ticket (admin)

post.description: Uploads 1–5 images to Cloudinary and inserts them as image-type messages in the ticket. Delivers each message over WebSocket to the user and the admin, then sends a push notification to the user. The ticket must not be closed. Accessible by super_admin, admin, and support roles.
post.summary: Send image reply to a support ticket (admin)

patch.description: Sets the ticket status to the given value. When resolving or closing, also stamps resolved_at and fires an in-app notification + push notification to the user. The action is recorded in the admin audit log. Accessible by super_admin, admin, and support roles.

parameters.description: New status. Must be one of: open | assigned | in_progress | waiting_user | resolved | closed
parameters.name: body

get.summary: Connect to live support chat as admin (WebSocket)

get.description: Returns a paginated list of all users. Supports filtering by active_role, status, and a search term that matches first_name, last_name, email, or phone_number. Soft-deleted users are excluded.

parameters.description: Filter by role: client, artisan, owner
parameters.name: role

parameters.description: Filter by status: approved, pending, suspended, probation, rejected
parameters.name: status

parameters.description: Search by first_name, last_name, email, or phone_number
parameters.name: search

get.description: Returns full profile of a single user including bank details where applicable. Role-specific data (artisan services, owner listings) will be added as those modules are built.

delete.description: Permanently deletes a user account. This is a hard delete — use with caution. Super admin only.

patch.description: Updates a user's account status. Valid values are approved, suspended, probation, rejected, pending.

get.description: Returns a paginated list of all withdrawals. Optionally filter by status: pending, processing, successful, failed.

parameters.description: Filter by status: pending, processing, successful, failed
parameters.name: status

get.description: Checks the live transfer status directly from Paystack for a given withdrawal. Automatically syncs the DB status if the webhook was missed.
Returns all campaigns for the authenticated user with pagination. Filter by status using ?status=active.

post.description: Creates a new ad campaign for the authenticated artisan or owner. Accepts multipart/form-data with an `image` file and JSON fields. For wallet payment the first day's charge is deducted immediately. For Paystack a checkout URL is returned.

get.description: Returns full details of a single campaign including daily charge history.
Returns daily view/click breakdown for a campaign over its lifetime.

patch.description: Allows the user to suspend, resume, or cancel a campaign. Admin auto-pauses are handled by the system.

get.description: Returns aggregate metrics and active campaigns for the authenticated user (artisan or owner).

post.description: Records a view or click event for an ad campaign. Used by the frontend when an ad is displayed or interacted with. Viewer is extracted from the auth token if provided.
post.summary: Record an ad impression or click

get.description: Returns the admin-configured daily ad prices for artisans and property owners, along with pre-computed totals per duration.
Returns all replies on a specific review, ordered oldest first.

post.description: The artisan can reply once to any review on their profile. The client can reply once after the artisan has responded. One reply per role per review.
A client can leave a rating and comment for an artisan after a completed and paid booking. Only one review per client per artisan — subsequent calls update the existing review.
post.summary: Leave a review for an artisan

get.description: Returns paginated reviews for a specific artisan, including a rating summary breakdown and any replies on each review.
Returns all active recurring availability windows for the authenticated artisan, optionally filtered by category.

post.description: Adds or updates a recurring weekly time window for the authenticated artisan within a service category. Multiple windows per day are allowed (e.g. morning + afternoon). If a window with the same artisan/category/weekday/start_time already exists it is updated; otherwise a new row is inserted.

parameters.description: weekday: 0=Sun…6=Sat; times in HH:MM format; category_id is a UUID
parameters.name: body

post.description: Creates or replaces a date-level override for the artisan. Set is_available=false to block a day (e.g. public holiday, personal day). Set is_available=true to force-open a day that would normally be off.
post.summary: Block or open a specific date

parameters.description: date in YYYY-MM-DD format; category_id is a UUID
parameters.name: body

delete.description: Deactivates a recurring availability window by ID. Existing confirmed bookings are NOT affected.

get.description: Returns the categories registered on the authenticated artisan's profile, with category details populated.

post.description: Registers a job category on the artisan's profile. An artisan can have at most 2 categories. The DB enforces this with a trigger.

delete.description: Removes a category from the artisan's profile. All portfolio images and services tied to that category are cascade-deleted. Will fail if any service under this category has active bookings.

get.description: Returns all portfolio images the authenticated artisan has uploaded for a given category.
get.summary: Get own portfolio images for a category

post.description: Uploads up to 5 sample-work images for one of the artisan's registered categories. Send as multipart/form-data with field name "images". The DB enforces a per-category limit of 5 images.
post.summary: Upload portfolio images for a category

delete.description: Deletes a single portfolio image and removes it from Cloudinary.

patch.description: Updates the caption or sort_order of an existing portfolio image.

get.description: Returns the platform-defined variation types for a job category. Artisans use these to configure their service pricing options.
get.summary: Get variation types for a category

get.description: Returns all services created by the authenticated artisan, optionally filtered by category_id.

post.description: Artisan creates a new service listing under one of their registered categories. The artisan must already have the category registered in artisan_categories.

delete.description: Permanently deletes a service and all its options. Will fail if the service is referenced by a confirmed or pending booking.

patch.description: Artisan updates the name, description, base price, or active status of one of their services.

get.description: Returns all pricing options configured for a service, grouped by variation type. Works for both the artisan owner and public clients.

post.description: Artisan adds a variation option to their service. Each option references a platform variation type (e.g. "Hair length") and carries the artisan's own label (e.g. "Extra long") and a price modifier on top of the base price.
post.summary: Add a pricing option to a service

delete.description: Removes a pricing option from a service.

patch.description: Artisan updates the label or price modifier of an existing service option.

get.description: Returns the categories registered on a specific artisan's profile, with category details populated.
Returns all portfolio images for a specific artisan and category.
get.summary: Get an artisan's portfolio images for a category (public)

get.description: Returns all active services for a specific artisan. Clients use this to browse before creating a booking.
Returns aggregated stats for the authenticated artisan's dashboard:
Returns a paginated list of artisans whose online status is TRUE.

patch.description: Allows an authenticated artisan to set their online or offline status. Only users with active_role 'artisan' and account status 'approved' can use this endpoint.

get.description: Returns the full public profile of a single artisan by their user UUID.

post.description: Authenticates a user with an Apple identity token. Creates a new account if none exists. Apple only sends email and name on the very first sign-in — the client should pass them in the request body as a fallback for subsequent logins.

parameters.description: Apple identity token. first_name, last_name, and email are only available on first sign-in from Apple.
parameters.name: body

post.description: Registers a device FCM token for the authenticated user. device_type must be android or ios.
Sends a password reset OTP to the user's email, phone number, or recovery email. Provide exactly one of the three fields.

patch.description: Resets the user's password using the OTP sent via forgot-password.

post.description: Authenticates a user with a Google ID token. Creates a new account if none exists, or links to an existing account matched by google_sub or email. Wallet and user_roles are created automatically for new users.

parameters.description: Google ID token and desired role (client, artisan, owner). Defaults to client if omitted.
parameters.name: body

post.description: Authenticates a user with email or phone number and password. Returns a JWT token.

parameters.description: Login credentials — provide either email or phone_number
parameters.name: body

post.description: Clears the Bearer cookie and logs the user out.

patch.description: Updates the authenticated user's password. Requires the current password for verification.

post.description: Issues a new JWT access token using a valid refresh token stored in the refresh_token cookie. Also rotates the refresh token (old one is revoked, new one is issued).
Resends a new OTP to the user's email or phone number. Only works if the contact is not yet verified.

Creates a new user account and sends an OTP for verification. Wallet is created automatically on registration. Username is required and must be unique.
If only email is provided, OTP is sent to email. If only phone_number is provided, OTP is sent to phone.
If both email and phone_number are provided, a first request (without otp_channel) returns status "choose_channel" — resend the full payload with otp_channel set to either "email" or "phone".

parameters.description: Registration payload. Provide email, phone_number, or both. If both, include otp_channel ('email' or 'phone'). active_role must be client, artisan, or owner
parameters.name: body

200.description: status=choose_channel — returned when both email and phone are provided but otp_channel is missing

delete.description: Soft-deletes the authenticated user's account. Requires password confirmation for local accounts.

post.description: Uploads or replaces the authenticated user's profile picture. Accepts multipart/form-data with an avatar file field.

get.description: Checks if a username is available. Uses a bloom filter — only hits the DB on a probable match.
Returns whether the authenticated user has an email and/or phone number on their account.
Returns the full profile of the authenticated user, including wallet info and all activated roles.

patch.description: Updates the authenticated user's editable profile fields: bio, username. Username can only be changed once every 30 days.

parameters.description: Fields to update — all optional, send only what you want to change
parameters.name: body

post.description: Adds a recovery email to the user's account and sends an OTP to verify it.

delete.description: Removes the recovery email from the authenticated user's account.

post.description: Confirms the OTP sent to the recovery email to activate it.

patch.description: Switches the user's active role. If switching to a role for the first time, that role is added to the user's role list. Issues a new JWT with the updated role.

parameters.description: Target role: client, artisan, or owner
parameters.name: body

post.description: Adds an email or phone number to an account that was created with only one contact.
Verifies the OTP sent to the newly added email or phone number.
Verifies the OTP sent to the previously unverified contact on the account.
Sends an OTP to the contact (email or phone) that was provided at signup but not yet verified.
Verifies the OTP sent to the user's email or phone number during registration. On success, issues a JWT and sends a role-specific welcome email.

get.description: Returns a list of all banks supported by Paystack (name and code only). Available to all roles.

post.description: Verifies a bank account number against a bank via Paystack. Available to artisan, client, and owner roles.

get.description: Returns a paginated list of bookings. Clients see bookings they created; artisans see bookings assigned to them. Optionally filter by status.

post.description: Creates a booking request for a specific artisan, date, and time slot. The slot must fall within the artisan's active availability window and must not already be booked. The booking starts in 'pending' status — the artisan must confirm before any payment is collected. Payment method is chosen at payment time (POST /bookings/{id}/pay), not here.

get.description: Returns the recurring weekly schedule and any upcoming date overrides for a specific artisan/category. Useful for displaying a calendar UI before choosing a date.
get.summary: Get an artisan's public availability schedule

get.description: Returns a day-by-day availability breakdown for an artisan within a given date range (max 60 days). For each date: checks the weekday recurring schedule, applies any date-level overrides, then subtracts confirmed/pending bookings to determine if the slot is still open.
get.summary: Get available slots for an artisan

parameters.description: End date YYYY-MM-DD (inclusive, max 60 days from from)
parameters.name: to

get.description: Returns full detail for a booking. The caller must be the client or the artisan on the booking.

patch.description: Either the client or the artisan can cancel a booking that is pending or confirmed. If payment was already made, a refund is triggered separately via the escrow/payment service.
Either the artisan or client can mark a booking as completed.
Artisan confirms a pending booking. The booking moves to 'confirmed' and the client is notified to complete payment. No money is collected here.
Artisan declines a pending booking. No payment was collected so no refund is needed.

post.description: Allows a client or artisan to file a dispute against the other party for a booking. The booking must be completed or awaiting client confirmation and must not already have an active dispute. Accepts multipart form data with an optional evidence field (max 5 images). Filing a dispute freezes the escrow payment pending admin resolution.

Initiates payment for a booking that has been confirmed by the artisan. Supports wallet (instant) and Paystack (redirect). The full amount is held in escrow and only released to the artisan after the booking is marked completed. Only callable by the client on the booking.
Either participant can end a call that is in ringing or accepted state. The other participant is notified via WebSocket and the Stream room is closed.

parameters.description: End request. reason: hangup | declined | missed | timeout | network_error | failed
parameters.name: body

get.description: Returns a paginated list of calls for the authenticated user. Optionally filter by context_type (booking|order) and context_id.

parameters.description: Filter by specific booking or order UUID
parameters.name: context_id

post.description:
Initiates an audio-only call between two participants of a booking or order.
context_type must be "booking" (client↔artisan) or "order" (client↔owner).
No phone numbers are ever exposed — Stream handles the media layer.
A push notification and WebSocket event are sent to the callee.
Issues a time-limited Stream token for the authenticated participant of a call. The caller must be either the caller or callee on the call.
post.summary: Get a Stream token for a call

get.description: Returns full details of a call. The caller must be a participant.

post.description: The callee accepts a ringing call. Both participants are notified via WebSocket.
The callee rejects a ringing call. The caller is notified via WebSocket.

get.description: Returns a paginated list of all conversations for the authenticated user, with the last message preview and unread count per conversation.

post.description: Creates a new conversation between a client and an artisan for a specific job, or retrieves the existing one if it already exists. Only the client who owns the job can start the conversation.

delete.description: Soft-deletes a conversation. Only the client (owner) who created it can delete it. The conversation and its messages remain in the database for audit purposes.

post.description: Uploads 1–5 images to Cloudinary and inserts each as an image message in the conversation. Both participants receive a WebSocket push for each image. The recipient also gets a push notification.

get.description: Returns paginated message history for a conversation. The caller must be a participant. Supports limit and offset query params.

patch.description: Marks all unread messages in a conversation (sent by the other party) as read. Also notifies the sender via WebSocket that their messages were read.

get.description:
Upgrades the connection to a WebSocket. The authenticated user is registered with the chat hub. All chat messaging, typing indicators, and read receipts happen over this connection.

**Incoming frames (client → server):**

Send a text message:
`{"type":"message","conversation_id":"<uuid>","content":"Hello!"}`

Mark messages as read:
`{"type":"read","conversation_id":"<uuid>"}`

Typing indicator:
`{"type":"typing","conversation_id":"<uuid>"}`

**Outgoing frames (server → client):**

New message: `{"type":"message","payload":{...Message}}`
Read receipt: `{"type":"read","payload":{"conversation_id":"...","reader_id":"..."}}`
Typing: `{"type":"typing","payload":{"conversation_id":"...","sender_id":"..."}}`
Error: `{"type":"error","payload":{"code":"...","message":"..."}}`
Returns a paginated list of properties that the authenticated client has saved.
Returns all booking disputes where the authenticated user is either the filer or the respondent, ordered by most recent. Supports pagination via page and limit query params.
Returns a paginated list of all job disputes filed by or against the authenticated user.

parameters.description: Filter by status: open, investigating, resolved_refund, resolved_release, dismissed
parameters.name: status

get.description: Returns all shortlet order disputes where the authenticated user is either the filer or the respondent, ordered by most recent. Supports pagination via page and limit query params.
Retrieves the support ticket and full message history for a dispute the authenticated user filed. Also marks any unread admin messages as read. Pass dispute_id and type as query params.

parameters.description: Dispute type: job, booking, or order (default: job)
parameters.name: type

post.description: Allows an authenticated user to file a dispute against a job they are party to. Evidence images can be included.

409.description: Dispute already exists for this job

get.description: Returns a paginated list of notifications for the authenticated user. Supports filtering by category and read status. The unread_count in the response always reflects the global unread count regardless of filters.

parameters.description: Filter by category: bookings, jobs, payments, reviews, system
parameters.name: category

parameters.description: Filter by read status: true or false
parameters.name: is_read

patch.description: Marks all unread notifications as read for the authenticated user.

delete.description: Permanently deletes a single notification. The notification must belong to the authenticated user.

patch.description: Marks a single notification as read. The notification must belong to the authenticated user.

get.description: Returns a paginated list of orders. Clients see orders they made; owners see orders for their properties. Filter by status optionally.

parameters.description: Filter by status: pending, confirmed, cancelled, checked_in, checked_out, completed, disputed
parameters.name: status

post.description: Creates a shortlet booking for a client. The order is created with status='pending' and payment_status='pending'. Payment is collected immediately via wallet or Paystack. On successful payment the order is automatically confirmed. Double-booking is prevented with a transaction-level lock.

parameters.description: payment_method: wallet | paystack; email only needed for Paystack if account has no email
parameters.name: body

post.description: Calculates and returns a full cost breakdown for a potential booking WITHOUT creating the order or charging the client. Use this to display a booking summary screen before the client confirms. Also validates date availability.

get.description: Returns full details for an order. The caller must be the client or the owner on the order.

patch.description: Cancels a pending or confirmed order. The client or owner can cancel. If payment was made, the full amount (including caution fee) is refunded to the client's wallet via escrow reversal. Orders with status checked_in or completed cannot be cancelled.
Owner marks the guest as checked in. Order must be confirmed and check-in date must be today or earlier.
patch.summary: Mark guest as checked in (owner only)

patch.description: Owner marks the guest as checked out. This completes the booking and releases the net payout (after platform commission) to the owner's wallet. The caution fee is released back to the client.
patch.summary: Mark guest as checked out — completes booking and releases escrow (owner only)

post.description: Allows a client or property owner to file a dispute for a shortlet order. The order must be confirmed, checked-in, or completed and must not already have an active dispute. Accepts multipart form data with an optional evidence field (max 5 images). Filing a dispute freezes the escrow funds pending admin resolution.

Allows a client to leave a review (rating + optional comment) for a property after their order is completed. One review per order is enforced. The property's avg_rating is updated automatically via a DB trigger.

get.description: Returns aggregated stats for the authenticated owner's dashboard:
Returns all properties (active, inactive, pending_review) belonging to the authenticated owner.

parameters.description: Filter by status: active, inactive, pending_review, suspended
parameters.name: status

get.description: Returns all properties with status='draft' for the authenticated owner, including the partial draft_data payload so the client can pre-fill the form.
Returns all addresses for the authenticated artisan.

post.description: Adds a home or work address for the authenticated artisan. Only one home address is allowed (enforced by DB constraint). Multiple work addresses are allowed; the first work address is automatically set as primary.

parameters.description: Address payload. address_type must be 'home' or 'work'. is_primary only applies to work addresses.
parameters.name: body

delete.description: Deletes a specific address for the authenticated artisan. Cannot delete a primary work address if other work addresses exist — set another as primary first.

patch.description: Partially updates a specific address for the authenticated artisan. Only provided fields are updated. Coordinates are updated together — both latitude and longitude must be provided if updating location.
Sets a work address as the primary work address for the authenticated artisan. Only work addresses can be set as primary.

get.description: Returns all bank accounts for the authenticated artisan, primary account first.

post.description: Adds a bank account for the authenticated artisan. The first account is automatically set as primary. All subsequent accounts must share the same account name as the primary (to prevent identity fraud). A Paystack transfer recipient is created for each account.

delete.description: Deletes a bank account. Cannot delete the only account or the primary account (set another as primary first).

patch.description: Marks a specific bank account as the artisan's primary payout account. The target account name must match the current primary account name.

get.description: Returns all addresses for the authenticated client.

post.description: Adds a home or work address for the authenticated client. Only one home address is allowed (enforced by DB constraint). Multiple work addresses are allowed; the first work address is automatically set as primary.

parameters.description: Address payload. address_type must be 'home' or 'work'. is_primary only applies to work addresses.
parameters.name: body

delete.description: Deletes a specific address for the authenticated client. Cannot delete a primary work address if other work addresses exist — set another as primary first.

patch.description: Partially updates a specific address for the authenticated client.
Sets a work address as the primary work address for the authenticated client. Only work addresses can be set as primary.

get.description: Returns all bank accounts for the authenticated client, primary account first.

post.description: Adds a bank account for the authenticated client. The first account is automatically set as primary. All subsequent accounts must share the same account name as the primary. A Paystack transfer recipient is created for each account.

delete.description: Deletes a bank account. Cannot delete the only account or the primary account (set another as primary first).

patch.description: Marks a specific bank account as the client's primary payout account. The target account name must match the current primary account name.

get.description: Returns all addresses for the authenticated owner.

post.description: Adds a home or work address for the authenticated owner. Only one home address is allowed (enforced by DB constraint). Multiple work addresses are allowed; the first work address is automatically set as primary.

parameters.description: Address payload. address_type must be 'home' or 'work'. is_primary only applies to work addresses.
parameters.name: body

delete.description: Deletes a specific address for the authenticated owner. Cannot delete a primary work address if other work addresses exist — set another as primary first.

patch.description: Partially updates a specific address for the authenticated owner.
Sets a work address as the primary work address for the authenticated owner. Only work addresses can be set as primary.

get.description: Returns all bank accounts for the authenticated owner, primary account first.

post.description: Adds a bank account for the authenticated owner. The first account is automatically set as primary. All subsequent accounts must share the same account name as the primary. A Paystack transfer recipient is created for each account.

delete.description: Deletes a bank account. Cannot delete the only account or the primary account (set another as primary first).

patch.description: Marks a specific bank account as the owner's primary payout account. The target account name must match the current primary account name.

get.description: Returns a paginated list of active properties. Supports rich filtering, full-text search, and sorting.

parameters.description: Filter by type: apartment, studio, 1_bedroom, etc.
parameters.name: property_type

parameters.description: Comma-separated list of required amenities e.g. WiFi,AC
parameters.name: amenities

parameters.description: Check-in date YYYY-MM-DD — filters to available properties
parameters.name: checkin

parameters.description: Check-out date YYYY-MM-DD — filters to available properties
parameters.name: checkout

parameters.description: Full-text search on name, description, city, street
parameters.name: search

parameters.description: Sort field: price_asc, price_desc, rating_desc, newest (default: newest)
parameters.name: sort

post.description: Allows an authenticated owner to create a new shortlet property listing. Images are uploaded via multipart form. Up to 5 images are accepted. The listing starts as 'active' by default.

parameters.description: One of: apartment, studio, 1_bedroom, 2_bedroom, 3_bedroom, 4_bedroom, 5_bedroom_plus, duplex, penthouse, villa, bungalow
parameters.name: property_type

post.description: Creates a draft listing that the owner can fill in incrementally and publish later. All fields are optional — save whatever the owner has typed so far. The listing will have status='draft' and will not appear in public searches until published.

get.description: Returns full details of a property, including its review summary. Clients can access active listings; owners can access their own regardless of status.

delete.description: Soft-deletes a property. Only the owner can delete it. Properties with active confirmed orders cannot be deleted.

patch.description: Allows the owner to update any field of their property listing. Send only the fields you want to change. Images that are not re-uploaded remain unchanged. Sending new images replaces all existing ones (max 5 total).

post.description: Creates or updates an availability date range for the property. Multiple non-overlapping windows are allowed (e.g. different months). If a range with the same from/to already exists it is updated in place.
post.summary: Set availability window for a property

parameters.description: Dates in YYYY-MM-DD, times in HH:MM (default 14:00 / 11:00)
parameters.name: body

post.description: Adds a date-level override that makes a day unavailable (e.g. personal use, maintenance). Cannot block a date that has a confirmed booking.

delete.description: Removes a previously blocked date override, making the day available again (subject to the active availability windows).
Deactivates an availability window. Does not affect existing confirmed orders.

get.description: Returns a day-by-day availability breakdown for a property over a date range (max 90 days). Each day indicates whether it is available, blocked by the owner, or already booked. Designed to power a calendar/date-picker UI.

parameters.description: End date YYYY-MM-DD (inclusive, max 90 days from from)
parameters.name: to

patch.description: Validates that the draft has all required fields and sets status to 'active', making it publicly searchable. Returns a 400 with a list of missing fields if validation fails so the client can prompt the owner to complete them.

get.description: Returns a paginated list of reviews for a property, including any replies. Also returns the aggregate rating summary (avg, count, breakdown by star).

post.description: Toggles the saved/bookmark status for a property. If not saved, it saves it. If already saved, it removes it. Only clients can save listings.
Allows the review author (client) or the property owner to reply to a review once each. Each party may only reply once. To reply as an owner, you must be the owner of the property being reviewed.

get.description: Returns a paginated list of shortlet conversations for the authenticated user (client or owner), with last-message preview and unread count.

post.description:
Creates (or retrieves) a conversation between the client and property owner for a confirmed order.
Only the client who owns the order can start the conversation.
The order must be in confirmed, checked_in, or completed status.
post.summary: Start or retrieve a shortlet conversation

delete.description: Soft-deletes a shortlet conversation. Only the client who created it can delete it.

post.description: Uploads 1–5 images to Cloudinary and inserts each as an image message. Both participants receive a WebSocket push per image.
post.summary: Send image message(s) in a shortlet conversation

get.description: Returns paginated messages for a shortlet conversation. Caller must be a participant.

patch.description: Marks all unread messages sent by the other party as read and notifies them via WebSocket.

get.description:
Upgrades the HTTP connection to WebSocket for shortlet (property) chat.
Participants are the client who booked and the property owner.

**Incoming frames (client → server):**
Send text: `{"type":"message","conversation_id":"<uuid>","content":"Hello!"}`
Mark read: `{"type":"read","conversation_id":"<uuid>"}`
Typing:    `{"type":"typing","conversation_id":"<uuid>"}`

**Outgoing frames (server → client):**
New message: `{"type":"message","payload":{...ShortletMessage}}`
Read receipt:`{"type":"read","payload":{"conversation_id":"...","reader_id":"..."}}`
Typing:      `{"type":"typing","payload":{"conversation_id":"...","sender_id":"..."}}`
Error:       `{"type":"error","payload":{"code":"...","message":"..."}}`
get.summary: Connect to the shortlet chat WebSocket

get.description: Returns all support tickets belonging to the authenticated user (client, artisan, or owner), paginated newest-first. Also returns unread admin message count per ticket.

post.description: Any authenticated platform user (client, artisan, or owner) can open a support ticket. Each ticket gets a unique ID and starts in "open" status.

parameters.description: Ticket details. category must be one of: payment_and_refund, booking_and_reservation, service_issues, disputes, account_and_verification, technical_issue, report_user_or_property, general_inquiry, other. priority: low | medium | high | urgent (default: medium).
parameters.name: body

get.description: Returns the full detail of one support ticket. The authenticated user must be the ticket owner.
Returns paginated messages for a ticket oldest-first. Also marks all unread admin messages as read. The authenticated user must own the ticket.

post.description: Uploads 1–5 images to Cloudinary and posts them as image-type messages in the ticket. The authenticated user must own the ticket and it must not be closed or resolved.
post.summary: Send image message(s) in a support ticket

patch.description: Marks all unread admin messages in the ticket as read. The authenticated user must own the ticket.

get.description: Returns the health status of the server and its dependencies (database and Redis). Returns 200 if all components are healthy, 503 if any are degraded.

post.description: Initialises a Paystack payment to top up the authenticated user's wallet. Returns a Paystack checkout URL. The wallet is credited automatically once the webhook confirms payment.

parameters.description: amount in Naira (min 100); email only required if account has none
parameters.name: body

get.description: Verifies the status of a Paystack payment by reference. Used as the redirect callback after Paystack checkout. Note: wallet crediting is handled by the webhook — this endpoint is informational only.

post.description: Initiates a direct Paystack transfer to the user's primary bank account. A ₦50 processing fee is deducted. The wallet is debited immediately; funds arrive once Paystack confirms the transfer. Requires a primary bank account to be set up first.

get.description: Returns a paginated list of the authenticated user's past withdrawals.

priority.description:
Priority is the ticket urgency level. Defaults to "high" when omitted.
Example: high

subject.description:
Subject is the ticket title shown in the support chat.
Defaults to "Dispute escalation — <type> dispute" when omitted.
Example: Regarding your incomplete job dispute

admin_notes.description:
AdminNotes is an optional internal note attached to the dispute
Example: Reviewing uploaded evidence from both parties
admin_notes.example: Reviewing uploaded evidence from both parties

status.description:
Status must be "open" or "investigating"
Example: investigating

reason.example: Artisan did not complete the agreed work

action.description: Action must be one of: refund_full, release_full, refund_partial, dismiss
